meta.section: Endpoint Management — Windows (Phase 1 of 7: Compliance policies)
meta.source: Microsoft Learn (learn.microsoft.com/intune) — Windows 10/11 compliance policy structure. NOTE: drafted from established Intune knowledge on 2026-07-03 while the Learn MCP was unavailable; re-verify setting labels/doc links on next pass.
generalNotes: These four rows are the setting GROUPS of a Windows 10/11 device compliance policy, not four separate policies. In practice you create one policy (Intune admin center > Devices > Compliance > Create policy > Windows 10 and later) and configure each group on the Compliance settings tab: Device Health, Device Properties, System Security, and Microsoft Defender for Endpoint.

Set the enforcement once for the whole policy on the 'Actions for noncompliance' tab (the default 'Mark device noncompliant' action; add a grace period and optional user email), then assign to the Windows device/user group. The master's per-row grace windows (~12h for OS version, ~24h for System Security) are the baseline intent — Intune schedules this action in days, so use the nearest equivalent.
Compliance only bites when paired with a Conditional Access 'require compliant device' grant — confirm that CA policy exists, or non-compliant devices won't actually be blocked.
Before assigning broadly, check the Devices > Compliance monitoring reports to see how many devices would flip to non-compliant (especially OS Version), and stage with a pilot group first.

requiredSettings.label: Grace period
requiredSettings.value: ~12 hours before a non-compliant device is blocked (set on Actions for noncompliance)

steps: Intune admin center > Devices > Compliance > (your Windows 10/11 policy) > Compliance settings > Device Properties.
On Actions for noncompliance, set the 'Mark device noncompliant' grace period to the baseline window.

Note (HIGH impact): any device on Windows 10 or an older Win 11 build is marked non-compliant and loses access after the grace period. Audit the fleet's OS versions and stage the rollout — this is the single most disruptive compliance setting.

requiredSettings.label: Device Security
requiredSettings.value: Firewall = Require; Trusted Platform Module (TPM) = Require; Antivirus = Require; Antispyware = Require

requiredSettings.label: Microsoft Defender
requiredSettings.value: Microsoft Defender Antimalware = Require; Real-time protection = Require; Security intelligence (signatures) up to date = Require

steps: Intune admin center > Devices > Compliance > (your Windows policy) > Compliance settings > System Security.
Under Device Security set Firewall, TPM, Antivirus, and Antispyware all to Require.
Under Microsoft Defender set Microsoft Defender Antimalware, Real-time protection, and signature-up-to-date all to Require.
Note (MEDIUM impact): devices with a security feature disabled or stale Defender signatures go non-compliant after the grace period; users may be prompted to update Defender / re-enable protection.

requiredSettings.label: Require Secure Boot to be enabled on the device
Encryption of data storage on device

steps: Intune admin center > Devices > Compliance > (your Windows policy) > Compliance settings > Device Health.
Set Require BitLocker = Require, Require Secure Boot = Require, Require code integrity = Require; on System Security set 'Encryption of data storage on device' = Require.

Note (MEDIUM impact): unencrypted devices go non-compliant; BitLocker may trigger a one-time encryption pass (temporary performance hit) and the user is prompted to save a recovery key. Pair with the BitLocker disk-encryption profile (Phase 3) so keys are escrowed to Entra/Intune.

requiredSettings.label: Require the device to be at or under the machine risk score
requiredSettings.value: Set the threshold per the baseline (Clear / Low / Medium / High) — e.g. Medium so Medium/High-risk devices are non-compliant

requiredSettings.label: Prerequisite
requiredSettings.value: The Intune ↔ Defender for Endpoint connector must be turned on (Endpoint security > Microsoft Defender for Endpoint), and devices onboarded — see Phase 2

steps: First confirm the MDE connection is On (Endpoint security > Microsoft Defender for Endpoint > connection settings) and devices are onboarded.
Intune admin center > Devices > Compliance > (your Windows policy) > Compliance settings > Microsoft Defender for Endpoint.
Set 'Require the device to be at or under the machine risk score' to the baseline threshold.

Note (MEDIUM impact): a device whose MDE risk score exceeds the threshold (active threats) is marked non-compliant and can lose resource access until remediated; clean devices are unaffected. Needs MDE Plan 1 (included in Business Premium).
